title: eugenia neoaustralis
intro: Eugenia neoaustralis, also known as the mountain apple or mountain guava, is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae. Native to New Caledonia, this plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The Eugenia neoaustralis plant has been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, fighting against infections, and supporting immune function. The leaves and fruits of the plant can be used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote digestive health and alleviate respiratory issues.
